pr.title: Initial Contribution guidelines and README formatting changes
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Added the project's initial CONTRIBUTING.md coding-style guide and simplified README formatting while linking readers to the new contribution guidelines. The guide documented expectations for PR submissions, especially naming, struct layout annotation, and conditional formatting. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Created CONTRIBUTING.md with a Melee Coding Style guide for pull requests.

Documented naming guidance: avoid naming unmatched functions based only on prior names, affix Melee functions with the file name, use lower camel casing for the descriptive portion, and upper camel casing for the function name itself except standard-library reimplementations.
Added concrete naming examples: lbVector_sin, lbArchive_Parse, Player_DoThing, Stage_GetLeftBlastZone, ftFox_LaserOnDeath.

Documented struct formatting guidance for Melee code: include offset-prefixed member names in larger structs such as Fighter-related structs, and remove copied struct members not used by current or existing code.
Documented conditional formatting guidance: make NULL checks explicit with ptr != NULL, and avoid dangling else/else if formatting unless the if body is also unbraced.
Updated README.md to link to CONTRIBUTING.md near the top and in the Contributions section.

Removed several standalone
spacing tags from README.md and changed the Contributions text from stylized wording to plain 'Pull Requests are welcome.'
smart_moves: Centralized contribution expectations in a dedicated CONTRIBUTING.md instead of scattering coding-style rules through README.md or PR comments.
Used positive and negative examples for structs and conditionals, which makes review expectations easier to apply consistently.
Included decomp-specific guidance rather than generic C style only, especially around unmatched function names, file-name affixes, and offset-prefixed struct fields.
Linked the guidelines from README.md in two visible places so new contributors can find them before opening PRs.
decomp_lessons: Do not assign semantic names to functions that have not been matched or understood; inherited names from other work should not be treated as proof.
For Melee code, function names should generally carry the source file/module affix to improve readability, even if asserts may suggest the original name was different.
Large decomp structs should preserve offset visibility in member names and comments, making layout verification and future matching easier.
When borrowing structs from another project, trim fields that are not represented in current code or existing code to avoid misleading layout assumptions.
Explicit NULL checks are preferred over terse pointer truthiness checks in this project's style.
Brace style should avoid mixed braced/unbraced if-else chains; either use braces consistently or keep both branches single-line/unbraced.
naming_conventions: Avoid naming a function unless it has been matched or understood.
Affix Melee-related functions with the file name for readability.
Use lower camel casing for the multiword descriptive part of function names.
Use upper camel casing for the function name itself, except for standard-library reimplementations.
Examples given by the PR: lbVector_sin, lbArchive_Parse, Player_DoThing, Stage_GetLeftBlastZone, ftFox_LaserOnDeath.
Prefix variables in larger Melee structs with their offset, e.g. x0_thing, x4_filler, x10_thing.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The only matching-related guidance in this slice is process/style guidance: do not name unmatched functions without understanding their functionality.
Struct layout should expose offsets in member names/comments, which supports layout-sensitive matching work.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that updated CONTRIBUTING.md coding style rules.
Find review comments citing explicit NULL checks or dangling else formatting.
Find PRs discussing function naming for unmatched or partially matched functions.
Find PRs discussing offset-prefixed struct fields or copied structs from other projects.
